Acrobatic.description: You get a +2 bonus on all Jump checks and Tumble checks.

Agile.description: You get a +2 bonus on all Balance checks and Escape Artist checks.

Alertness.description:
You get a +2 bonus on all Listen checks and Spot checks.

The master of a familiar gains the benefit of the Alertness feat whenever the familiar is within arm’s reach.

Animal Affinity.description: You get a +2 bonus on all Handle Animal checks and Ride checks.

Armor Proficiency (Heavy).description: See Armor Proficiency (light). Fighters, paladins, and clerics automatically have Armor Proficiency (heavy) as a bonus feat.

Armor Proficiency (Light).description: When you wear a type of armor with which you are proficient, the armor check penalty for that armor applies only to Balance, Climb, Escape Artist, Hide, Jump, Move Silently, Sleight of Hand, and Tumble checks.

Armor Proficiency (Medium).description: See Armor Proficiency (light). Fighters, barbarians, paladins, clerics, druids, and bards automatically have Armor Proficiency (medium) as a bonus feat.

Athletic.description: You get a +2 bonus on all Climb checks and Swim checks.

Augment Summoning.description: Each creature you conjure with any summon spell gains a +4 enhancement bonus to Strength and Constitution for the duration of the spell that summoned it.

Blind-Fight.description: In melee, every time you miss because of concealment, you can reroll your miss chance percentile roll one time to see if you actually hit. You take only half the usual penalty to speed for being unable to see.

Brew Potion.description: You can create a potion of any 3rd-level or lower spell that you know and that targets one or more creatures.

Cleave.description: If you deal a creature enough damage to make it drop, you get an immediate, extra melee attack against another creature within reach.

Combat Casting.description: You get a +4 bonus on Concentration checks made to cast a spell or use a spell-like ability while on the defensive or while you are grappling or pinned.

Combat Expertise.description: When you use the attack action or the full attack action in melee, you can take a penalty of as much as -5 on your attack roll and add the same number as a dodge bonus to your Armor Class.

Combat Reflexes.description: You may make a number of additional attacks of opportunity equal to your Dexterity bonus.

Craft Magic Arms and Armor.description: You can create any magic weapon, armor, or shield whose prerequisites you meet.

Craft Rod.description: You can create any rod whose prerequisites you meet.

Craft Staff.description: You can create any staff whose prerequisites you meet.

Craft Wand.description: You can create a wand of any 4th-level or lower spell that you know.

Craft Wondrous Item.description: You can create any wondrous item whose prerequisites you meet.

Deceitful.description: You get a +2 bonus on all Disguise checks and Forgery checks.

Deflect Arrows.description: Once per round when you would normally be hit with a ranged weapon, you may deflect it so that you take no damage from it.

Deft Hands.description: You get a +2 bonus on all Sleight of Hand checks and Use Rope checks.

Diehard.description: When reduced to between -1 and -9 hit points, you automatically become stable.

Diligent.description: You get a +2 bonus on all Appraise checks and Decipher Script checks.

Dodge.description: During your action, you designate an opponent and receive a +1 dodge bonus to Armor Class against attacks from that opponent.

Empower Spell.description: All variable, numeric effects of an empowered spell are increased by one-half.

Endurance.description: You gain a +4 bonus on many checks and saves related to endurance and environmental hazards.

Enlarge Spell.description: You can alter a spell with a range of close, medium, or long to increase its range by 100%.

Eschew Materials.description: You can cast any spell that has a material component costing 1 gp or less without needing that component.

Exotic Weapon Proficiency.description: You make attack rolls with the selected exotic weapon as if it were a simple or martial weapon.

Extend Spell.description: An extended spell lasts twice as long as normal.

Extra Turning.prerequisites: Ability to turn or rebuke creatures
Extra Turning.description: Each time you take this feat, you can use your ability to turn or rebuke creatures four more times per day than normal.

Extra Wild Shape.description: Each time you take this feat, you can use your wild shape ability two additional times per day.

Extra Rings.description: You can wear two additional magic rings beyond the normal limit. Requires Forge Ring and caster level 12. Choose rings on the Feat Options tab under Feats & Features.

Far Shot.description: When you use a projectile weapon, its range increment increases by one-half.

Forge Ring.description: You can create any ring whose prerequisites you meet.

Great Cleave.prerequisites: Str 13, Cleave, Power Attack, base attack bonus +4
Great Cleave.description: This feat works like Cleave, except that there is no limit to the number of times you can use it per round.

Great Fortitude.description: You get a +2 bonus on all Fortitude saving throws.

Greater Spell Focus.description: Add +1 to the Difficulty Class for all saving throws against spells from the school of magic you select.

Greater Spell Penetration.description: You get a +2 bonus on caster level checks made to overcome a creature’s spell resistance.

Greater Two-Weapon Fighting.prerequisites: Dex 19, Improved Two-Weapon Fighting, Two-Weapon Fighting, base attack bonus +11
Greater Two-Weapon Fighting.description: You get a third attack with your off-hand weapon, albeit at a -10 penalty.

Greater Weapon Focus.prerequisites: Proficiency with selected weapon, Weapon Focus with selected weapon, fighter level 8th
Greater Weapon Focus.description: You gain a +1 bonus on all attack rolls you make using the selected weapon.

Greater Weapon Specialization.prerequisites: Proficiency with selected weapon, Greater Weapon Focus, Weapon Focus, Weapon Specialization, fighter level 12th
Greater Weapon Specialization.description: You gain a +2 bonus on all damage rolls you make using the selected weapon.

Heighten Spell.description: A heightened spell has a higher spell level than normal (up to a maximum of 9th level).

Improved Bull Rush.description: When you perform a bull rush you do not provoke an attack of opportunity from the defender.

Improved Counterspell.description: When counterspelling, you may use a spell of the same school that is one or more spell levels higher than the target spell.

Improved Critical.prerequisites: Proficient with weapon, base attack bonus +8
Improved Critical.description: When using the weapon you selected, your threat range is doubled.

Improved Disarm.description: You do not provoke an attack of opportunity when you attempt to disarm an opponent.

Improved Feint.description: You can make a Bluff check to feint in combat as a move action.

Improved Grapple.description: You do not provoke an attack of opportunity when you make a touch attack to start a grapple.

Improved Initiative.description: You get a +4 bonus on initiative checks.

Improved Overrun.description: When you attempt to overrun an opponent, the target may not choose to avoid you.

Improved Precise Shot.prerequisites: Dex 19, Point Blank Shot, Precise Shot, base attack bonus +11
Improved Precise Shot.description: Your ranged attacks ignore the AC bonus granted to targets by anything less than total cover.

Improved Shield Bash.description: When you perform a shield bash, you may still apply the shield’s shield bonus to your AC.

Improved Sunder.description: When you strike at an object held or carried by an opponent, you do not provoke an attack of opportunity.

Improved Trip.description: You do not provoke an attack of opportunity when you attempt to trip an opponent while you are unarmed.

Improved Turning.prerequisites: Ability to turn or rebuke creatures
Improved Turning.description: You turn or rebuke creatures as if you were one level higher than you are.

Improved Two-Weapon Fighting.prerequisites: Dex 17, Two-Weapon Fighting, base attack bonus +6
Improved Two-Weapon Fighting.description: In addition to the standard single extra attack, you get a second attack with your off-hand weapon.

Improved Unarmed Strike.description: You are considered to be armed even when unarmed.

Investigator.description: You get a +2 bonus on all Gather Information checks and Search checks.

Iron Will.description: You get a +2 bonus on all Will saving throws.

Leadership.description: Having this feat enables the character to attract loyal companions and devoted followers.

Lightning Reflexes.description: You get a +2 bonus on all Reflex saving throws.

Magical Aptitude.description: You get a +2 bonus on all Spellcraft checks and Use Magic Device checks.

Manyshot.prerequisites: Dex 17, Point Blank Shot, Rapid Shot, base attack bonus +6
Manyshot.description: As a standard action, you may fire two arrows at a single opponent within 30 feet.

Martial Weapon Proficiency.description: You make attack rolls with the selected martial weapon normally.

Martial Stance.description:
You have mastered the fundamentals of a martial discipline, and you are now able to master one of its stances.

When you gain this feat, you can select any stance from a discipline in which you already know at least one maneuver. You must meet the normal prerequisite of the stance. Your martial adept level for using this maneuver is equal to your levels in martial adept classes (if any) + 1/2 your levels in other classes.

You can choose this feat more than once. When you take this feat again, you gain knowledge of a new stance. You do not have to choose a stance from the same discipline you selected the first time, but you must know at least one martial maneuver from the discipline of the stance you choose. (ToB)

Martial Study.description:
By studying the basics of a martial discipline, you learn to focus your ki and perfect the form needed to use a maneuver. As a result, you gain the use of a combat maneuver.

When you gain this feat, you must choose a discipline of martial maneuvers, such as Desert Wind. The key skill for the chosen discipline becomes a class skill for all of your classes (current and future). Select any maneuver from the chosen discipline for which you meet the prerequisite. If you have martial adept levels, this maneuver becomes one of your maneuvers known. If you do not have martial adept levels, you can use this maneuver once per encounter as a martial adept with an initiator level equal to 1/2 your character level. If you do not have martial adept levels when you take this feat, and you later gain a level in a class that grants maneuvers known, these new maneuvers can only be used once per encounter and have no recovery method. If you later gain levels in a martial adept class (crusader, swordsage, or warblade), you use the recovery method for maneuvers learned as a result of those class levels, but your previous maneuvers (gained through this feat or through prestige class levels) do not gain a recovery method.

A maneuver learned through this feat cannot be exchanged for a different maneuver if you are a crusader, swordsage, or warblade. Once you choose a maneuver with this feat, you cannot change it.

You can take this feat up to three times. Each time you take it after the first, you gain one of two benefits. You can choose a new discipline, gaining one of its maneuvers and its key skill as a class skill, as described above. Alternatively, you can choose a maneuver from a discipline to which you have already gained access by means of this feat. In either case, you must meet the maneuver's prerequisite. (ToB)

Maximize Spell.description: All variable, numeric effects of a maximized spell are maximized. +3 Spell Levels

Mobility.description: You get a +4 dodge bonus to Armor Class against attacks of opportunity caused when you move out of or within a threatened area.

Mounted Archery.description: The penalty you take when using a ranged weapon while mounted is halved.

Mounted Combat.description: Once per round when your mount is hit in combat, you may attempt a Ride check to negate the hit.

Natural Spell.description: You can complete the verbal and somatic components of spells while in a wild shape.

Negotiator.description: You get a +2 bonus on all Diplomacy checks and Sense Motive checks.

Nimble Fingers.description: You get a +2 bonus on all Disable Device checks and Open Lock checks.

Persistent Spell.description:
Spells with a fixed or personal range can have their duration increased to 24 hours.

 Spells of instantaneous duration cannot be affected by this feat, nor can spells whose effects are discharged. You don't need to maintain concentration on persistent detect spells (such as detect magic or detect thoughts) for you to be aware of the mere presence or absence of the subject detected, but gaining additional information requires concentration as normal. A persistent spell uses up a spell slot six levels higher than the spell's actual level.

Persuasive.description: You get a +2 bonus on all Bluff checks and Intimidate checks.

Point Blank Shot.description: You get a +1 bonus on attack and damage rolls with ranged weapons at ranges of up to 30 feet.

Power Attack.description: On your action, before making attack rolls for a round, you may choose to subtract a number from all melee attack rolls and add the same number to all melee damage rolls.

Precise Shot.description: You can shoot or throw ranged weapons at an opponent engaged in melee without taking the standard -4 penalty.

Quick Draw.description: You can draw a weapon as a free action instead of as a move action.

Quicken Spell.description: Casting a quickened spell is a swift action.

Rapid Reload.description: The time required for you to reload your chosen type of crossbow is reduced to a free action or move action.

Rapid Shot.description: You can get one extra attack per round with a ranged weapon.

Ride-By Attack.description: When you are mounted and use the charge action, you may move and attack as if with a standard charge and then move again.

Run.description: When running, you move five times your normal speed (or four times if wearing heavy armor).

Scribe Scroll.description: You can create a scroll of any spell that you know.

Self-Sufficient.description: You get a +2 bonus on all Heal checks and Survival checks.

Shield Proficiency.description: You can use a shield and take only the standard penalties.

Shot on the Run.prerequisites: Dex 13, Dodge, Mobility, Point Blank Shot, base attack bonus +4
Shot on the Run.description: When using the attack action with a ranged weapon, you can move both before and after the attack.

Silent Spell.description: A silent spell can be cast with no verbal components.

Simple Weapon Proficiency.description: You make attack rolls with simple weapons normally.

Skill Focus.description: You get a +3 bonus on all checks involving the chosen skill.

Snatch Arrows.prerequisites: Dex 15, Deflect Arrows, Improved Unarmed Strike
Snatch Arrows.description: When using the Deflect Arrows feat you may catch the weapon instead of just deflecting it.

Spell Focus.description: Add +1 to the Difficulty Class for all saving throws against spells from the chosen school of magic.

Spell Mastery.description: Each time you take this feat, choose a number of spells equal to your Intelligence modifier that you already know. You can prepare these spells without referring to a spellbook.

Spell Penetration.description: You get a +2 bonus on caster level checks made to overcome a creature’s spell resistance.

Spirited Charge.prerequisites: Ride 1 rank, Mounted Combat, Ride-By Attack
Spirited Charge.description: When mounted and using the charge action, you deal double damage with a melee weapon (or triple damage with a lance).

Spring Attack.prerequisites: Dex 13, Dodge, Mobility, base attack bonus +4
Spring Attack.description: When using the attack action with a melee weapon, you can move both before and after the attack.

Stealthy.description: You get a +2 bonus on all Hide checks and Move Silently checks.

Still Spell.description: A stilled spell can be cast with no somatic components.

Stunning Fist.prerequisites: Dex 13, Wis 13, Improved Unarmed Strike, base attack bonus +8
Stunning Fist.description: You must declare that you are using this feat before you make your attack roll. Stunning Fist forces a foe to make a Fortitude saving throw or be stunned for 1 round.

Tower Shield Proficiency.description: You can use a tower shield and suffer only the standard penalties.

Track.description: To find tracks or to follow them for 1 mile requires a successful Survival check.

Trample.description: When you attempt to overrun an opponent while mounted, your target may not choose to avoid you.

Two-Weapon Defense.description: When wielding a double weapon or two weapons, you gain a +1 shield bonus to your AC.

Two-Weapon Fighting.description: Your penalties on attack rolls for fighting with two weapons are reduced.

Weapon Finesse.description: With a light weapon, rapier, whip, or spiked chain, you may use your Dexterity modifier instead of your Strength modifier on attack rolls.

Weapon Focus.prerequisites: Proficiency with selected weapon, base attack bonus +1
Weapon Focus.description: You gain a +1 bonus on all attack rolls you make using the selected weapon.

Weapon Specialization.prerequisites: Proficiency with selected weapon, Weapon Focus with selected weapon, fighter level 4th
Weapon Specialization.description: You gain a +2 bonus on all damage rolls you make using the selected weapon.

Whirlwind Attack.prerequisites: Dex 13, Int 13, Combat Expertise, Dodge, Mobility, Spring Attack, base attack bonus +4
Whirlwind Attack.description: When you use the full attack action, you can give up your regular attacks and instead make one melee attack at your full base attack bonus against each opponent within reach.

Widen Spell.description: You can alter a burst, emanation, line, or spread shaped spell to increase its area by 100%.

Extra Smite.description: Each time you take this feat, you can use your smite ability one additional time per day.

Acrobatic Strike.description: If you succeed in using Tumble to avoid an opponent's attack of opportunity, you gain a +4 bonus on the next attack that you make against that foe as long as the attack occurs before the end of your current turn. (PHB2)

Arcane Flourish.description: As a swift action, you can expend a prepared spell or spell slot to grant your next Perform check a competence bonus equal to 1 + the level of the spell or slot. (PHB2)

Arcane Accompaniment.prerequisites: Perform 4 ranks, Arcane Flourish, arcane caster level 1st, bardic music
Arcane Accompaniment.description: As a swift action, you can expend a prepared spell or a spell slot to extend the duration of your bardic music ability after you stop performing. (PHB2)

Arcane Thesis.prerequisites: Knowledge (arcana) 9 ranks, ability to cast arcane spells
Arcane Thesis.description: Choose one arcane spell to be your thesis. +2 CL with it. Other metamagic (not Heighten) costs 1 less slot level. (PHB2)

Arcane Toughness.description: Immediate when reduced to 0 or fewer hp: expend prepared spell or slot to heal that many points (stabilizes you). (PHB2)

Arcane Consumption.prerequisites: Arcane Toughness, Toughness, arcane caster level 6th
Arcane Consumption.description: 1/day swift: your next spell gets +4 save DC. You suffer -4 Con for 12 hours and become fatigued. (PHB2)

Armor Specialization.prerequisites: Proficiency with selected armor type, base attack bonus +12
Armor Specialization.description: Choose one medium or heavy armor type. With masterwork or magic version of it: DR 2/-. (PHB2)

Battle Dancer.description: While using bardic music to grant an ally a bonus, you gain +2 morale on attack rolls if you move at least 1 square before attacking. (PHB2)

Bonded Familiar.description: 1/day, if within 30 ft you and your familiar can have one of you absorb the hit point damage from a single attack meant for the other. (PHB2)

Bounding Assault.prerequisites: Dex 13, Dodge, Mobility, Spring Attack, base attack bonus +12
Bounding Assault.description: Spring Attack against two designated foes (no AoO from either). You may make a second attack at -5. (PHB2)

Rapid Blitz.prerequisites: Dex 13, Bounding Assault, Dodge, Mobility, Spring Attack, base attack bonus +18
Rapid Blitz.description: You can make a third attack as part of a Spring Attack. (PHB2)

Brutal Strike.prerequisites: Str 13, Power Attack, base attack bonus +6
Brutal Strike.description: Power Attack with bludgeoning weapon: foe Fort saves (DC 10 + your extra PA damage) or sickened 1 round. (PHB2)

Combat Acrobat.description: DC 20 Balance to avoid being knocked prone by an effect. DC 15 Balance to ignore up to 4 squares of difficult terrain. (PHB2)

Combat Familiar.description: Your familiar does not provoke attacks of opportunity when entering a foe's square while holding the charge for a touch spell. (PHB2)

Lurking Familiar.description: Your familiar can hide in your square. (PHB2)

Combat Tactician.description: Free action designate a foe. If you start your turn not threatening each other, +2 on melee damage against it this turn. (PHB2)

Cometary Collision.prerequisites: Str 13, Improved Bull Rush, Power Attack
Cometary Collision.description: Ready vs a charger: you charge them gaining +2 attack and +4 damage, and they lose the benefits of charging against you. (PHB2)

Companion Spellbond.description: Share spells with your animal companion out to 30 feet. You may cast touch-only spells targeting your companion at short range. (PHB2)

Crossbow Sniper.prerequisites: Proficiency with hand, heavy or light crossbow, Weapon Focus (chosen crossbow), base attack bonus +1, skirmish or sneak attack
Crossbow Sniper.description: With chosen crossbow (Weapon Focus): +1/2 Dex bonus on damage. Skirmish or sneak attack range increases to 60 ft. (PHB2)

Cunning Evasion.description: If you completely avoid an area attack with evasion, you may immediate action Hide + 5-ft step into suitable cover. (PHB2)

Dampen Spell.description: Immediate action after identifying a spell being cast: expend a spell or slot to reduce the target's save DC by the level you expended. (PHB2)

Deadeye Shot.prerequisites: Point Blank Shot, Precise Shot, base attack bonus +4, skirmish or sneak attack
Deadeye Shot.description: Ready a ranged attack vs a target. If an ally strikes it first, it loses Dex bonus to AC against your attack. (PHB2)

Defensive Sweep.description: An adjacent opponent that begins its turn and does not move provokes an attack of opportunity from you immediately after its turn ends. (PHB2)

Elven Spell Lore.description: You gain +2 on dispel checks. You may change a spell's energy type (acid, cold, electricity, or fire) as a free action when casting. (PHB2)

Fade into Violence.description: When a foe attempts to attack you, make a Bluff check opposed by its Sense Motive. If you win, it strikes a chosen ally within 5 feet instead. (PHB2)

Fiery Fist.prerequisites: Dex 13, Wis 13, Improved Unarmed Strike, Stunning Fist, base attack bonus +8
Fiery Fist.description: Expend one Stunning Fist use: your unarmed strikes deal +1d6 fire damage for a number of rounds equal to your Wisdom modifier. (PHB2)

Fiery Ki.prerequisites: Dex 13, Wis 13, Fiery Fist, Improved Unarmed Strike, Stunning Fist, base attack bonus +8
Fiery Ki.description: Expend a Stunning Fist use to cloak yourself in flame. Any creature that strikes you takes 1d6 fire damage. (PHB2)

Force of Personality.description: You add your Charisma modifier (instead of your Wisdom modifier) to Will (PHB2)

Ki Blast.prerequisites: Dex 13, Wis 13, Fiery Fist, Improved Unarmed Strike, Stunning Fist, base attack bonus +8
Ki Blast.description: Expend two Stunning Fist uses as a standard action to throw a ball of ki energy (60 ft ranged touch attack) dealing 2d6 + Wis mod fire damage. (PHB2)

Flay.description: When using Power Attack with a slashing weapon against an unarmored or lightly armored target, the target takes a -2 penalty on attack rolls and skill checks for 1 round. (PHB2)

Grenadier.description: You gain a +1 bonus on attack and damage rolls with splash weapons (alchemist's fire, holy water, thunderstones, etc.). (PHB2)

Gloom Razor.prerequisites: Shadow Blade, Hide 9 ranks, base attack bonus +6, two Shadow Hand maneuvers
Gloom Razor.description:
Tactical feat. The teachings of the Shadow Hand discipline allow you to confuse your enemies. Your attacks and parries are infused with the stuff of shadow. Through careful training and discipline, you learn to use your ki to inhibit a foe's fighting ability with your shadowy nature.

Lingering Gloom: To use this option, your opponent must miss you due to concealment. On your next turn, you can make a Hide check opposed by your foe's Spot check as a swift action. If this check succeeds, you gain the benefit of an invisibility spell against that foe until the end of your turn.

Moving Shadows: To use this option, you must deal damage to an enemy with a melee attack and move at least 10 feet during your turn. On your next turn, you can treat that enemy as flat-footed against the first melee attack you make in that round.

Shadow Slip: To use this option, you must make a successful melee attack against an opponent you flank. On your next turn, you can make a DC 20 Tumble check if you are adjacent to your foe. If this check succeeds, as a free action you can move to any square adjacent to your opponent without provoking attacks of opportunity from any opponent. (ToB)

Hindering Opportunist.description: When a foe provokes an attack of opportunity from you, you can choose to perform the aid another action instead of attacking. (PHB2)

Stalwart Defense.prerequisites: Combat Reflexes, Hindering Opportunist, base attack bonus +9
Stalwart Defense.description: Foes that attack your allies while adjacent to you provoke an aid another action from you. (PHB2)

Indomitable Soul.description: You roll twice and take the better result on Will saves against fear and mind-affecting attacks. (PHB2)

Insightful Reflexes.description: You add your Intelligence modifier (instead of your Dexterity modifier) to Reflex saves. (Complete Adventurer)

Intimidating Strike.description: As a standard action make a melee attack. If it hits, you may immediately attempt to demoralize the target with Intimidate as a free action. (PHB2)

Keen-Eared Scout.prerequisites: Listen 6 ranks, Alertness or Skill Focus (Listen)
Keen-Eared Scout.description: A successful Listen check reveals additional information (distance, direction, number of creatures, etc.) as appropriate. (PHB2)

Leap of the Heavens.description: You do not double the DC for standing long jumps. You gain a +5 bonus on Jump checks made after a running start. (PHB2)

Lunging Strike.description: As a standard action, you can make a single melee attack with 5 feet greater reach than normal. (PHB2)

Mad Foam Rager.description: Once per rage or frenzy, you can delay the effects of a single spell or attack until the end of your rage. (PHB2)

Master Manipulator.description: You can use Diplomacy to feint in combat or to make a creature's attitude toward you one step worse. (PHB2)

Melee Evasion.description: While fighting defensively, you can negate one melee attack against you per round as an immediate action (opposed attack roll at -4). (PHB2)

Melee Weapon Mastery.prerequisites: Proficiency with selected weapon, Weapon Focus with selected weapon
Melee Weapon Mastery.description: Choose bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing. You gain +2 on attack and damage rolls with weapons that deal the chosen damage type. (PHB2)

Crushing Strike.prerequisites: Proficiency with a bludgeoning weapon, Weapon Focus (bludgeoning), Melee Weapon Mastery (bludgeoning), Weapon Specialization (bludgeoning), base attack bonus +14
Crushing Strike.description: Full attack with bludgeoning weapon: each successful hit grants a cumulative +1 bonus on attack rolls against that foe until the end of your turn. (PHB2)

Driving Attack.prerequisites: Proficiency with selected piercing weapon, Weapon Focus (piercing), Melee Weapon Mastery (piercing), Weapon Specialization (piercing), base attack bonus +14
Driving Attack.description: As a full-round action, make a single attack with a piercing weapon. If it hits, you can immediately make a bull rush attempt against the target. (PHB2)

Slashing Flurry.prerequisites: Proficiency with selected slashing weapon, Weapon Focus (slashing), Melee Weapon Mastery (slashing), Weapon Specialization (slashing), base attack bonus +14
Slashing Flurry.description: Full attack with a slashing weapon: you gain one additional attack at your highest base attack bonus. (PHB2)

Weapon Supremacy.prerequisites: Proficiency with selected weapon, Greater Weapon Focus (selected), Greater Weapon Specialization (selected), Weapon Focus (selected), Weapon Specialization (selected), Melee Weapon Mastery (type), fighter level 18th
Weapon Supremacy.description: With the chosen weapon you gain multiple benefits including increased critical range/multiplier and the ability to ignore some damage reduction. (PHB2)

Overwhelming Assault.description: An adjacent foe that does not attack you on its turn grants you a +4 bonus on attack rolls against it until the start of its next turn. (PHB2)

Penetrating Shot.prerequisites: Str 15, Point Blank Shot, base attack bonus +10
Penetrating Shot.description: As a standard action make a single ranged attack that targets every creature in a 60-foot line. Make one attack roll and compare it to each target's AC. (PHB2)

Ranged Weapon Mastery.prerequisites: Proficiency with selected weapon, Weapon Focus (selected), Weapon Specialization (selected), base attack bonus +8
Ranged Weapon Mastery.description: Choose a ranged weapon type. You gain +1 on attack and damage rolls with it, and these bonuses also apply to all similar weapons (e.g. all bows). (PHB2)

Robilar's Gambit.description: You can grant any threatening foe a +4 bonus on its attack and damage rolls against you, but any attack it makes against you provokes an attack of opportunity from you. (PHB2)

Shield Specialization.description: You gain an additional +1 shield bonus to your Armor Class when using a shield. (PHB2)

Active Shield Defense.description: When fighting defensively with a shield you do not take the normal penalty on attacks of opportunity. Total defense with a shield still allows you to threaten and make AoOs at -4. (PHB2)

Agile Shield Fighter.prerequisites: Proficiency with shields, Improved Shield Bash, Shield Specialization
Agile Shield Fighter.description: When making a shield bash and an armed strike in the same round, the penalties are only -2 on each attack. (PHB2)

Shield Sling.prerequisites: Proficiency with shields, Improved Shield Bash, Shield Specialization, base attack bonus +9
Shield Sling.description: You can throw your shield as a ranged weapon (20 ft range increment) that returns to you at the end of the round. (PHB2)

Shield Ward.description: You apply your shield's bonus to your touch AC and to checks or saves made to resist bull rush, disarm, grapple, overrun, and trip attempts. (PHB2)

Short Haft.prerequisites: Proficiency with selected weapon, Weapon Focus with selected weapon, base attack bonus +3
Short Haft.description: You can use a reach weapon to attack adjacent foes without the normal -4 penalty. (PHB2)

Spectral Skirmisher.description: While invisible or incorporeal you gain a +2 bonus on attack and damage rolls, and your attacks ignore 5 points of damage reduction. (PHB2)

Spell-Linked Familiar.description: Your familiar can cast a single spell of 4th level or lower that you know once per day as a spell-like ability (caster level equals your arcane caster level). (PHB2)

Steadfast Determination.description: You can use your Constitution modifier in place of your Wisdom modifier when making Will saves. (PHB2)

Telling Blow.description: When you score a critical hit, you deal your skirmish or sneak attack damage in addition to the normal critical hit damage. (PHB2)

Trophy Collector.description: You can create trophies from defeated enemies. A worn trophy grants a +1 morale bonus on attack rolls or on checks with one chosen skill. (PHB2)

Tumbling Feint.description: After you successfully use Tumble to avoid an attack of opportunity from a foe, you gain a +5 bonus on the next feint attempt against that foe this turn. (PHB2)

Two-Weapon Pounce.prerequisites: Dex 15, Two-Weapon Fighting, base attack bonus +6
Two-Weapon Pounce.description: When charging while wielding two weapons, you can attack with both weapons at the end of the charge (normal two-weapon fighting penalties apply). (PHB2)

Two-Weapon Rend.prerequisites: Dex 15, Two-Weapon Fighting, base attack bonus +11
Two-Weapon Rend.description: If you hit the same opponent with both of your weapons in the same round, you deal an extra 1d6 + 1-1/2 times your Strength modifier damage. (PHB2)

Vatic Gaze.description: You can use detect magic at will. As a standard action you can use Sense Motive to determine the highest-level spell or spell-like ability a visible opponent has available. (PHB2)

Versatile Unarmed Strike.description: You may choose to deal bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage with your unarmed strikes (declare the type before the attack). (PHB2)

Vexing Flanker.description: You gain a +4 bonus on attack rolls when flanking (instead of the normal +2). (PHB2)

Adaptable Flanker.prerequisites: Combat Reflexes, Vexing Flanker, base attack bonus +4
Adaptable Flanker.description: As a swift action designate one opponent. You count as occupying any square you threaten for purposes of flanking that opponent (for you and your allies). (PHB2)

Wanderer's Diplomacy.prerequisites: Halfling or 4 ranks in Bluff, Diplomacy, and Sense Motive
Wanderer's Diplomacy.description: You gain a +2 bonus on Bluff, Diplomacy, and Sense Motive checks and several special social abilities while traveling. (PHB2)

Water Splitting Strike.prerequisites: Dex 13, Wis 13, Improved Unarmed Strike, Stunning Fist, base attack bonus +9
Water Splitting Strike.description: When you successfully strike a creature that possesses damage reduction, you gain a +4 bonus on the damage roll for that attack. (PHB2)

Ritual Blessing.description: You gain access to good-aligned rituals (Anoint, Cleanse, Ward) whose effects scale with your Knowledge (religion) ranks. (PHB2)

Ritual Blood Bonds.description: You gain access to blood-bond rituals (Blood Brothers, Vengeful Bonds of Brotherhood) that provide morale bonuses in combat. (PHB2)

Ritual Transference.prerequisites: Knowledge (arcana) or Knowledge (religion) 4 ranks, any item creation feat
Ritual Transference.description: You can transfer XP from other participants into a magic item you are crafting and allow participants to meet spell and other prerequisites for the item. (PHB2)

Combat Focus.description: After your first successful attack in an encounter you enter a state of combat focus lasting 10 + number of combat form feats rounds. While focused you gain +2 on Will saves (+4 with 3 or more combat form feats). (PHB2)

Combat Stability.prerequisites: Wis 13, Combat Focus, base attack bonus +3
Combat Stability.description: While maintaining combat focus you gain +4 on checks or rolls to resist bull rush, disarm, grapple, overrun, and trip (+8 with 3+ combat form feats). (PHB2)

Combat Defense.prerequisites: Dex 13, Wis 13, Combat Focus, Dodge, base attack bonus +6
Combat Defense.description: While focused you can change the target of your Dodge feat as an immediate action. With 3+ combat form feats you gain an extra +1 dodge bonus to AC against the Dodge target. (PHB2)

Combat Vigor.prerequisites: Wis 13, Combat Focus, base attack bonus +9
Combat Vigor.description: While maintaining combat focus you gain fast healing 2 (fast healing 4 with 3 or more combat form feats). (PHB2)

Combat Awareness.prerequisites: Wis 13, Blind-Fight, Combat Focus, base attack bonus +12
Combat Awareness.description: While focused you learn the current hit point totals of all adjacent creatures. With 3+ combat form feats you gain blindsight out to 5 feet. (PHB2)

Combat Strike.prerequisites: Wis 13, Combat Focus, any two other combat form feats, base attack bonus +15
Combat Strike.description: End your combat focus as a swift action to gain a bonus on attack and damage rolls equal to your total number of combat form feats for the rest of your turn. (PHB2)

Divine Armor.prerequisites: Divine caster level 5th, ability to turn or rebuke undead
Divine Armor.description: As a swift action you can expend a turn or rebuke undead attempt to gain damage reduction 5/- until the start of your next turn. (PHB2)

Divine Fortune.prerequisites: Divine caster level 5th, ability to turn or rebuke undead
Divine Fortune.description: As an immediate action you can expend a turn or rebuke attempt to gain a +4 bonus on your next saving throw (lasts until used or the start of your next turn). (PHB2)

Divine Justice.prerequisites: Ability to turn or rebuke undead
Divine Justice.description: Expend a turn/rebuke to mark a foe. The next time it strikes you in melee, record the damage. Your next successful melee attack against it deals the greater of its damage to you or your normal damage. (PHB2)

Divine Metamagic.prerequisites: Ability to turn undead or rebuke undead
Divine Metamagic.description: When you take this feat, choose a metamagic feat that you have. This feat applies only to that metamagic feat. As a free action, you can channel turn or rebuke undead uses to apply that metamagic feat to divine spells you know. Spend one turn or rebuke attempt, plus one additional attempt for each level the metamagic feat increases the spell. The spell slot does not change. This feat may be taken multiple times; each time, choose a different metamagic feat. (Complete Divine)

Divine Ward.prerequisites: Ability to turn or rebuke undead
Divine Ward.description: Spend 10 minutes and a turn attempt to create a 24-hour ward with one willing ally, allowing you to cast touch spells on that ally at short range by expending another turn attempt. (PHB2)

Profane Aura.prerequisites: Divine caster level 9th, ability to rebuke undead
Profane Aura.description: As a standard action expend a rebuke attempt to create a 60-foot-radius mist that grants concealment and gives mindless undead in the area a +2 deflection bonus to AC. (PHB2)

Sacred Healing.description: As a swift action expend a turn attempt to gain +5 on Heal checks and +2 per die on damage healed by any conjuration (healing) spells you cast this turn. (PHB2)

Sacred Purification.prerequisites: Sacred Healing, ability to turn undead
Sacred Purification.description: As a swift action expend a turn attempt to heal all living creatures within 60 feet for 1d8 + your Charisma modifier and deal the same amount of damage to undead in the area. (PHB2)

Sacred Radiance.prerequisites: Divine caster level 9th, ability to turn undead
Sacred Radiance.description: As a standard action expend a turn attempt to bathe yourself in light that provides bright illumination in a 60-foot radius, gives nonevil creatures +2 morale on saves vs fear/poison/disease/death, and gives evil creatures -2 on saves vs fear. Lasts 10 minutes. (PHB2)

Celestial Sorcerer Heritage.description: You gain a bonus on saves against electricity and petrification equal to the number of celestial sorcerer heritage feats you possess. Add protection from evil to your list of 1st-level sorcerer spells known. (PHB2)

Celestial Sorcerer Aura.description: As a standard action expend a spell slot to create a 20-foot aura of menace. Hostile creatures must Will save or take -2 on attack rolls, checks, and saves for 24 hours (or until they successfully hit you). (PHB2)

Celestial Sorcerer Lance.description: As a standard action expend a spell slot to create a 60-foot line of energy that deals 1d8 damage per slot level to evil creatures only (Reflex half). (PHB2)

Celestial Sorcerer Wings.description: Expend a spell slot as a standard action to gain the ability to fly at twice your base land speed (good maneuverability) for 1 round per slot level. (PHB2)

Celestial Sorcerer Lore.prerequisites: Celestial Sorcerer Heritage, any two other celestial sorcerer heritage feats, sorcerer level 1st
Celestial Sorcerer Lore.description: Add magic circle against evil, tongues, and teleport to your list of spells known (gained when you reach the appropriate spell level). (PHB2)

Infernal Sorcerer Heritage.description: You gain a bonus on saves against fire and poison equal to the number of infernal sorcerer heritage feats you possess. When summoning evil outsiders with conjuration (summoning), you gain +2 caster level. (PHB2)

Infernal Sorcerer Eyes.description: As a swift action expend a spell slot to see perfectly in any darkness out to the limit of your line of sight for 10 minutes per slot level. (PHB2)

Infernal Sorcerer Howl.description: Expend a spell slot to create a 30-foot cone of sonic energy dealing 2d6 damage per slot level (Fortitude half). (PHB2)

Infernal Sorcerer Resistance.description: You gain resistance to acid and resistance to cold equal to the total number of infernal sorcerer heritage feats you possess. (PHB2)

Blistering Spell.description: Metamagic (fire descriptor spells only): the spell deals an extra 2 points of fire damage per spell level. Creatures that fail their save take a -2 penalty on attack rolls and checks for 1 round. Uses a slot one level higher. (PHB2)

Earthbound Spell.description: Metamagic: you place the spell in an adjacent square as a trap (Search/Disable DC 25 + level). It triggers when a creature enters the square. Uses a slot two levels higher. (PHB2)

Flash Frost Spell.description: Metamagic (cold descriptor area spells only): the spell deals an extra 2 cold damage per level. The area is covered with slippery ice for 1 round (Balance DC 10 or 20 or fall prone). Uses a slot one level higher. (PHB2)

Imbued Summoning.description: Metamagic: when you cast a summoning spell you can also cast a touch-range spell of 3rd level or lower; the summoned creature gains the benefit of that spell. Uses a slot one level higher. (PHB2)

Smiting Spell.description: Metamagic: transfer a touch spell into a weapon you hold. The next successful strike with that weapon discharges the spell. Must be used within 1 minute. Uses a slot one level higher. (PHB2)

Blood-Spiked Charger.prerequisites: Str 13, proficiency with armor spikes and shield spikes, Power Attack, Weapon Focus (armor spikes), Weapon Focus (shield spikes), base attack bonus +6
Blood-Spiked Charger.description: Tactical feat granting special maneuvers and bonuses when charging with spiked armor and shield. (PHB2)

Combat Cloak.prerequisites: Dex 15, Int 13, Combat Expertise, Dodge, base attack bonus +6
Combat Cloak.description: Tactical feat granting special combat maneuvers using a cloak (trip, disarm, and more). (PHB2)

Combat Panache.prerequisites: Bluff 8 ranks, Intimidate 8 ranks, Perform 8 ranks
Combat Panache.description: Tactical feat granting charismatic combat maneuvers (improved feints, demoralize, etc.). (PHB2)

Einhander.description: Tactical feat granting one-handed weapon combat maneuvers. (PHB2)

Mad Alchemist.description: Tactical feat granting special combat maneuvers with thrown alchemical items. (PHB2)

Shadow Blade.description:
In the course of your training in the Shadow Hand discipline, you learn to use your natural agility and speed to augment your attacks with certain weapons.

While you are in a Shadow Hand stance and attack with one of the discipline's preferred weapons, you can add your Dexterity modifier as a bonus on melee damage for attacks made with the weapon.

Shadow Blade can be used in place of Weapon Finesse to qualify for a feat, prestige class, or other special ability. If this substitution allows you to gain a benefit that normally applies to all finesse weapons (those described in the Weapon Finesse feat description), it instead applies only to the Shadow Hand discipline's preferred weapons. (ToB)

Shadow Striker.description: Tactical feat granting stealthy combat maneuvers while using Hide and Move Silently. (PHB2)

Arcane Strike.prerequisites: Ability to cast 3rd-level arcane spells, base attack bonus +4
Arcane Strike.description: You can channel arcane energy into your melee attacks. Prerequisites: Ability to cast 3rd-level arcane spells, base attack bonus +4. Benefit: When you activate this feat (a free action that does not provoke an attack of opportunity), you can channel arcane energy into a melee weapon, your unarmed strike, or natural weapons. You must sacrifice one of your spells for the day (of 1st level or higher) to do this, but you gain a bonus on all your attack rolls for 1 round equal to the level of the spell sacrificed, as well as extra damage equal to 1d4 points × the level of the spell sacrificed. The bonus you add to your attack rolls from this feat cannot be greater than your base attack bonus. For example, Yarren the bladesinger has a base attack bonus of +11 and the ability to cast 4th-level arcane spells. On his turn, he chooses to sacrifice one of his 4th-level spells for the day, marking it off as if he had cast it. Until his next turn, Yarren gains an extra +4 bonus on his attack rolls and an extra 4d4 points of damage with a single melee weapon of his choice (his rapier). (Complete Warrior)

Arterial Strike.prerequisites: Sneak attack ability, base attack bonus +4
Arterial Strike.description: Your sneak attacks target large blood vessels, leaving wounds that cause massive blood loss. Prerequisites: Sneak attack ability, base attack bonus +4. Benefit: If you hit with a sneak attack, you may choose to forgo +1d6 of extra sneak attack damage to deliver a wound that won’t stop bleeding. Each wound caused in this manner saps an extra 1 point of damage per round from the victim, until the victim receives the benefit of a DC 15 Heal check or any cure spell or other magical healing. Wounds from multiple arterial strikes result in cumulative bleeding loss (two successful arterial strikes cause an extra 2 points of damage per round until healed). You may deliver only one bleeding wound per successful sneak attack. (Complete Warrior)

Axiomatic Strike.description: You can turn your fist into an instrument of law. Prerequisites: Ki strike (lawful), Stunning Fist. Benefit: Against a chaotic opponent, you can make an unarmed attack that does an extra 2d6 points of damage. You must declare that you are using this feat before you make your attack roll (thus, a failed attack ruins the attempt). Each attempt counts as one of your uses of the Stunning Fist feat for the day. Creatures immune to stunning can be affected by this extra damage. (Complete Warrior)

Clever Wrestling.prerequisites: Small or Medium size, Improved Unarmed Strike
Clever Wrestling.description: You have a better than normal chance to escape or wriggle free from a big creature’s grapple or pin. Prerequisites: Small or Medium size, Improved Unarmed Strike. Benefit: When your opponent is larger than Medium, you gain a circumstance bonus on your grapple check to escape a grapple or pin. The size of the bonus depends on your opponent’s size, according to the following table. Opponent Size Bonus Large +2 Huge +4 Gargantuan +6 Colossal +8 (Complete Warrior)

Dash.description: You can move faster than normal. Benefit: If you are wearing light armor or no armor and are carrying a light load, your speed is 5 feet faster. (Complete Warrior)

Defensive Throw.prerequisites: Dex 13, Combat Reflexes, Dodge, Improved Trip, Improved Unarmed Strike
Defensive Throw.description: You can use your opponent’s weight, strength, and momentum against her, deflecting her attack and throwing her to the ground. Prerequisites: Dex 13, Combat Reflexes, Dodge, Improved Trip, Improved Unarmed Strike. Benefit: If the opponent you have chosen to use your Dodge feat against attacks you and misses, you can make an immediate trip attack against that opponent. This attempt counts against your allowed attacks of opportunity in the round. (Complete Warrior)

Destructive Rage.description: You can shatter barriers and objects when enraged. Prerequisites: Rage or frenzy ability. Benefit: While you are in a rage or frenzy, you gain a +8 bonus on any Strength checks you make to break down doors or break inanimate, immobile objects. (Complete Warrior)

Eagle Claw Attack.prerequisites: Wis 13, Improved Sunder, Improved Unarmed Strike
Eagle Claw Attack.description: Your superior insight allows you to strike objects with impressive force. Prerequisites: Wis 13, Improved Sunder, Improved Unarmed Strike. Benefit: When you make an unarmed strike against an object, you may add your Wisdom bonus to the damage dealt to the object. (Complete Warrior)

Eyes in the Back of Your Head.description: Attackers do not gain the usual +2 bonus on their attack rolls when flanking you. This feat grants no effect whenever you are attacked without benefit of your Dexterity modifier to Armor Class, such as when you are flat-footed. You may still be sneak attacked when flanked. (Complete Warrior)

Faster Healing.description: Heal hit points and ability damage faster than normal (Complete Warrior)

Favored Power Attack.prerequisites: Favored enemy ability, Power Attack, base attack bonus +4
Favored Power Attack.description: You are able to deal more damage against your favored enemies. Prerequisites: Favored enemy ability, Power Attack, base attack bonus +4. Benefit: When you use the Power Attack feat against a favored enemy, you may subtract a number from your melee attack rolls and add twice that number to your melee damage rolls, if you attack with a weapon in two hands, add three times the number. The normal restrictions of the Power Attack feat apply. (Complete Warrior)

Fists of Iron.prerequisites: Improved Unarmed Strike, Stunning Fist, base attack bonus +2

Fleet of Foot.description: Make one direction change during a run or charge (Complete Warrior)

Flick of the Wrist.prerequisites: Dex 17, Sleight of Hand 5 ranks, Quick Draw
Flick of the Wrist.description: Cause opponent to be flat-footed for one attack (Complete Warrior)

Flying Kick.prerequisites: Str 13, Jump 4 ranks, Improved Unarmed Strike, Power Attack
Flying Kick.description: You literally leap into battle, dealing devastating damage. Prerequisites: Str 13, Jump 4 ranks, Improved Unarmed Strike, Power Attack. Benefit: When fighting unarmed and using the charge action, you deal an extra 1d12 points of damage with your unarmed attack. (Complete Warrior)

Greater Resiliency.description: Your extraordinary resilience to damage increases. Prerequisites: Damage reduction as a class feature or innate ability. Benefit: Your damage reduction increases by 1. if it would normally rise thereafter with level, it does so at its previous rate, adding the +1 normally. For example, a 13th-level barbarian has damage reduction 3/–. By taking this feat, he raises it to 4/–. When he reaches 16th level, his damage reduction becomes 5/– and at 19th level, it becomes 6/–. You may not take this feat more than once. This feat has no effect on the type of weapon or damage that overcomes your damage reduction. If you have more than one form of damage reduction, choose which to increase when you take this feat. (Complete Warrior)

Hamstring.prerequisites: Sneak attack ability, base attack bonus +4
Hamstring.description: You can wound your opponents’ legs, hampering their movement. Prerequisites: Sneak attack ability, base attack bonus +4. Benefit: If you hit with a melee sneak attack, you may choose to forgo 2d6 points of extra sneak attack damage to reduce your opponent’s base speed by half. This speed reduction ends after 24 hours have passed or a successful DC 15 Heal check or the application of any cure spell or other magical healing is made. Creatures immune to sneak attack damage and creatures with no legs or more than four legs can’t be slowed down with a hamstring attack. It takes two successful hamstring attacks to affect quadrupeds. Other speeds (fly, burrow, and so on) aren’t affected. You may use this ability once per round. (Complete Warrior)

Hold the Line.description: Make attack of opportunity against charging foe (Complete Warrior)

Improved Familiar.prerequisites: Ability to acquire a new familiar, compatible alignment, sufficient arcane spellcaster level and base attack bonus
Improved Familiar.description: This feat allows spellcasters to acquire a new familiar from a nonstandard list, but only when they could normally acquire a new familiar (see Familiars, page 52 of the Player’s Handbook). This feat was originally presented on page 200 of the Dungeon Master’s Guide; the description here provides new alternatives for arcane spellcasters who want familiars to stand beside them in battle. Prerequisite: Ability to acquire a new familiar, compatible alignment, sufficiently high arcane spellcaster level, and base attack bonus. Benefit: When choosing a familiar, the creatures listed below are also available to the spellcaster. The spellcaster may choose a familiar with an alignment up to one step away on each of the alignment axes (lawful through chaotic, good through evil). For example, a chaotic good spellcaster could acquire a neutral familiar, A lawful neutral spellcaster could acquire a neutral good familiar. The spellcaster must have at least the arcane spellcaster level and base attack bonus indicated below in order to acquire the familiar. Improved familiars otherwise use the rules presented on pages 52 and 53 of the Player’s Handbook. Granted Abilities: In addition to their own special qualities, all familiars grant their masters the Alertness feat, the benefit of an empathic link, and the ability to share spells with the familiar. Improved Evasion (Ex): If a familiar is exposed to any effect that normally allows it to attempt a Reflex saving throw for half damage, it rakes no damage with a successful saving throw and only half damage on a failed save. (Complete Warrior)

Improved Favored Enemy.prerequisites: Favored enemy ability, base attack bonus +5
Improved Favored Enemy.description: You know how to hit your favored enemies where it hurts. Prerequisites: Favored enemy ability, base attack bonus +5. Benefit: You deal an extra 3 points of damage to your favored enemies. This benefit stacks with any existing favored enemy bonus gained from another class. (Complete Warrior)

Instantaneous Rage.description: Your rage begins at any time you wish, even when it’s not your turn or when you’re surprised. You can activate your rage as a free action in response to another’s action. Thus, you can gain the benefits of rage in time to prevent or ameliorate an undesirable event. For example, you can choose to enter a rage when an enemy attacks you, or casts a spell at you (to gain the benefits of a higher Constitution or your bonus on Will saves) before you know the results of the attack. You must be aware of the attack, but you may be flat-footed. (Complete Warrior)

Intimidating Rage.description: Your rage engenders fear in your opponents. Prerequisites: Rage or frenzy ability. Benefit: While you are raging, you designate a single foe within 30 feet of you that you can attempt to demoralize as a free action (see the Intimidate skill, page 76 of the Players Handbook). A foe that you successfully demoralize remains shaken for as long as you continue to rage. You may only use this feat against a single foe in any particular encounter. (Complete Warrior)

Karmic Strike.description: You have learned to strike when your opponent is most vulnerable—the same instant your opponent strikes you. Prerequisites: Dex 13, Combat Expertise, Dodge. Benefit: You can make an attack of opportunity against an opponent that hits you in melee. On your action, you choose to take a –4 penalty to your Armor Class in exchange for the ability to make an attack of opportunity against any creature that makes a successful melee attack or melee touch attack against you. The opponent that hits you must be in your threatened area, and this feat does not grant you more attacks of opportunity than you are normally allowed in a round. You specify on your turn that you are activating this feat, and the change to your Armor Class and your ability to make these special attacks of opportunity last until your next turn. (Complete Warrior)

Kiai Shout.description: You can bellow forth a shout that strikes terror into your enemies. Prerequisites: Cha 13, base attack bonus +1. Benefit: Making a kiai shout is a standard action. Opponents who can hear your shout and who are within 30 feet of you may become shaken for 1d6 rounds. The kiai shout affects only opponents with fewer Hit Dice or levels than you have. An opponent in the affected area can resist the effect with a successful Will save (DC 10 + 1/2 your character level + your Cha modifier). You can use the benefit of this feat three times per day. (Complete Warrior)

Monkey Grip.description: You can use melee weapons one size category larger than you are with a –2 penalty on the attack roll, but the amount of effort it takes you to use the weapon does not change. For instance, a Large longsword (a one-handed weapon for a large creature) is considered a two-handed weapon for a Medium creature that does not have this feat. For a Medium creature that has this feat, it is still considered a one-handed weapon. You can wield a larger light weapon as a light weapon, or a larger two-handed weapon in two hands. You cannot wield a larger weapon in your off hand, and you cannot use this feat with a double weapon. (Complete Warrior)

Pain Touch.prerequisites: Wis 15, Stunning Fist, base attack bonus +2
Pain Touch.description: You cause intense pain in an opponent with a successful stunning attack. Prerequisites: Wis 15, Stunning Fist, base attack bonus +2. Benefit: Victims of a successful stunning attack are subject to such debilitating pain that they are nauseated for 1 round following the round they are stunned. Creatures that are immune to stunning attacks are also immune to the effect of this feat, as are any creatures that are more than one size category larger than the feat user. (Complete Warrior)

Pin Shield.description: You know how to get inside your opponent’s guard by pinning his shield out of the way. Prerequisites: Two-Weapon Fighting, base attack bonus +4. Benefit: This feat can be used only when fighting against an opponent who is using a shield and who is your size or one size category bigger or smaller than you. When making a full attack action, you may give up all your off-hand attacks, if you do, you momentarily pin your 119 opponent’s shield with your off-hand weapon, and all your remaining attacks during the round are made with your primary weapon (with the normal penalties for fighting with two weapons), and your foe gains no Armor Class benefit from her shield until the end of your action. You cannot use this feat if you are fighting with only one weapon. (Complete Warrior)

Roundabout Kick.prerequisites: Str 15, Improved Unarmed Strike, Power Attack
Roundabout Kick.description: You can follow up on a particularly powerful unarmed attack with a mighty kick, spinning in a complete circle before landing the kick. Prerequisites: Str 15, Improved Unarmed Strike, Power Attack. Benefit: If you score a critical hit on an unarmed attack, you can immediately make an additional unarmed attack against the same opponent, using the same attack bonus that you used for the critical hit roll. For example, Ember the 15th-level monk can make three unarmed attacks in a round, at base attack bonuses of+11, +6, and +1. if she scores a critical hit on her second attack, she can make an additional attack using her +6 base attack bonus. She then makes her third attack (at +1) as normal. (Complete Warrior)

Swarmfighting.prerequisites: Small size, Dex 13, base attack bonus +1
Swarmfighting.description: You and allies with this feat can coordinate melee attacks against a single target and are adept at fighting side by side in close quarters. Prerequisites: Small size, Dex 13, base attack bonus +1. Benefit: You can occupy the same 5-foot square in combat with any other allied Small creature that also possesses the Swarmfighting feat at no penalty. When you engage a Medium or larger creature in melee, and at least one other ally with the Swarmfighting feat threatens the target, you gain a +1 morale bonus on the attack roll. This bonus increases by +1 for each additional ally beyond the first with the Swarmfighting feat that threatens the same target. The total morale bonus imparted to your attack roll cannot exceed your Dexterity bonus. (Complete Warrior)

Throw Anything.prerequisites: Dex 15, proficiency with weapon, base attack bonus +2
Throw Anything.description: You can throw a melee weapon you are proficient with as if it were a ranged weapon. The range increment of weapons used in conjunction with this feat is 10 feet. (Complete Warrior)

Zen Archery.description: Your intuition guides your hand when you use a ranged weapon. Prerequisites: Wis 13, base attack bonus +1. Benefit: You can use your Wisdom modifier instead of your Dexterity modifier when making a ranged attack roll. DIVINE FEATS (Complete Warrior)

Earth's Embrace.prerequisites: Str 15, Improved Grapple or improved grab, Improved Unarmed Strike
Earth's Embrace.description: Extra damage while pinning an opponent (Complete Warrior)

Freezing the Lifeblood.prerequisites: Wis 17, Improved Unarmed Strike, Stunning Fist, base attack bonus +10
Freezing the Lifeblood.description: Declare that you are using this feat before you make your attack roll (thus, a missed attack roll ruins the attempt). Against a humanoid opponent, you can make an unarmed attack that deals no damage but has a chance of paralyzing your target. If your attack is successful, your target must attempt a Fortitude saving throw (DC 10 + 1/2 your character level + your Wis modifier), if the target fails this saving throw, it is paralyzed for 1d4+1 rounds. Each attempt to paralyze an opponent counts as one of your uses of the Stunning Fist feat for the day. Creatures immune to stunning cannot be paralyzed in this manner. (Complete Warrior)

Close-Quarters Fighting.description: You gain an attack of opportunity whenever an enemy attempts to grapple you, even if the enemy has a feat or special ability that would normally bypass the attack, if you deal damage with this attack, the enemy fails to Stan the grapple unless it has the Improved Grapple feat or a special ability such as improved grab. If the enemy has such an ability, you may add the damage you deal as a bonus on your opposed check to resist being grappled. This feat does not give you extra attacks of opportunity during a round or allow you to make an attack of opportunity when you would be denied one for being surprised, helpless, or in a similar situation. For example, an ogre attempts to grapple Tordek. Tordek gains an attack of opportunity, hits, and causes damage. Since the ogre does not have any sort of grappling special ability or feat, it fails to start a grapple. Then an ankheg—a creature with the improved grab special ability—attempts to grapple Tordek. He takes an attack of opportunity, hits, and deals 10 points of damage to the creature. Tordek then adds +10 to his opposed check to resist being grappled. (Complete Warrior)

Defensive Strike.description: If an opponent attacks you and misses while you are using the total defense action, you can attack that opponent on your next turn with a +4 bonus on your attack roll. You gain no bonus against an opponent that does not attack you or against an opponent that attacks and hits you. (Complete Warrior)

Extend Rage.description: Each of the uses of your rage or frenzy ability lasts an additional 5 rounds beyond its normal duration. (Complete Warrior)

Extra Rage.description: You rage or frenzy two more times per day than you otherwise could. (Complete Warrior)

Extra Smiting.description: When you take this feat, you gain two extra attempts to smite per day. Use whatever smite ability you have (the paladin’s smite evil ability or the hunter of the dead’s ability to smite undead, for example). (Complete Warrior)

Extra Stunning.description: You gain the ability to make three extra stunning attacks per day. (Complete Warrior)

Greater Two-Weapon Defense.prerequisites: Dex 19, Improved Two-Weapon Fighting, Two-Weapon Defense, Two-Weapon Fighting, base attack bonus +11
Greater Two-Weapon Defense.description: When wielding two weapons (not including natural weapons or unarmed strikes), you gain a +3 shield bonus to your AC. When you are fighting defensively or using the total defense action, this shield bonus increases to +6. (Complete Warrior)

Improved Buckler Defense.description: When you attack with a weapon in your offhand, you may still apply your buckler’s shield bonus to your Armor Class. (Complete Warrior)

Improved Combat Expertise.prerequisites: Int 13, Combat Expertise, base attack bonus +6
Improved Combat Expertise.description: When you use the Combat Expertise feat to improve your Armor Class, the number you subtract from your attack roll and add to your AC can be any number that does not exceed your base attack bonus. (Complete Warrior)

Improved Mounted Archery.prerequisites: Ride 1 rank, Mounted Archery, Mounted Combat
Improved Mounted Archery.description: The penalty you take when using a ranged weapon if your mount is taking a double move is eliminated, and the penalty for using a ranged weapon when your mount is running is lessened from –4 to –2. You can attack at any time during your mount’s move. (Complete Warrior)

Improved Rapid Shot.prerequisites: Manyshot, Point Blank Shot, Rapid Shot
Improved Rapid Shot.description: When using the Rapid Shot feat, you may ignore the –2 penalty on all your ranged attack rolls. (Complete Warrior)

Improved Toughness.description: You gain a number of hit points equal to your current Hit Dice. Each time you gain a HD (such as by gaining a level), you gain 1 additional hit point. If you lose a HD (such as by losing a level), you lose 1 hit point permanently. (Complete Warrior)

Improved Two-Weapon Defense.prerequisites: Dex 17, Two-Weapon Defense, Two-Weapon Fighting, base attack bonus +6
Improved Two-Weapon Defense.description: When wielding two weapons (not including natural weapons or unarmed strikes), you gain a +2 shield bonus to your Armor Class. When you are fighting defensively or using the total defense action, this shield bonus increases to +4. (Complete Warrior)

Improved Weapon Familiarity.description: You can treat all the exotic weapons associated with your race as martial weapons rather than as exotic weapons. A weapon is treated as being associated with a race if the race’s name appears as part of the weapon’s name, such as the elven thinblade (see Chapter 4 of this book) or the dwarven urgrosh. (Complete Warrior)

Phalanx Fighting.prerequisites: Proficiency with a heavy shield, base attack bonus +1
Phalanx Fighting.description: If you are using a heavy shield and a light weapon, you gain a +1 bonus to your Armor Class. In addition, if you are within 5 feet of an ally who is also using a heavy shield and light weapon and who also has this feat, you may form a shield wall. A shield wall provides an extra +2 bonus to AC and a +1 bonus on Reflex saves to all eligible characters participating in the shield wall. For example, a single character with this feat gains a +1 bonus to his AC. If two or more characters who all know this feat are adjacent, they each gain an extra +2 bonus to AC (for a total of+3) and a +1 bonus on Reflex saves. (Complete Warrior)

Power Critical.prerequisites: Weapon Focus with weapon, base attack bonus +4
Power Critical.description: When using the weapon you selected, you gain a +4 bonus on the roll to confirm a threat. (Complete Warrior)

Prone Attack.prerequisites: Dex 15, Lightning Reflexes, base attack bonus +2
Prone Attack.description: You can make an attack from the prone position and take no penalty on your attack roll, if your attack roll is successful, you may regain your feet immediately as a free action. Opponents gain no bonus on melee attacks against you while you are prone. (Complete Warrior)

Ranged Disarm.prerequisites: Dex 15, Point Blank Shot, Precise Shot, base attack bonus +5
Ranged Disarm.description: Choose one type of ranged weapon with which you are proficient. You can make a disarm attempt with this weapon as long as your target is within 30 feet. (Complete Warrior)

Ranged Pin.prerequisites: Dex 15, Point Blank Shot, Precise Shot, base attack bonus +5
Ranged Pin.description: You can perform a ranged grapple attempt against an opponent by pinning a bit of its clothing to a nearby surface. The target must be within 5 feet of a wall, tree, or other surface in which a thrown weapon or projectile can he stuck and must be wearing some sort of clothing, armor, or other accoutrement. You must succeed on a ranged attack (not a ranged touch attack) and then win an opposed grapple check (your size modifier and the target’s size modifiers still apply). To break free, the victim must make a DC 15 Strength check or a DC 15 Escape Artist check as a standard action. (Complete Warrior)

Rapid Stunning.prerequisites: Combat Reflexes, Stunning Fist, base attack bonus +6
Rapid Stunning.description: You may use one additional stunning attack (or other special attack that counts against your daily limit of stunning attacks) per round. (Complete Warrior)

Sharp-Shooting.prerequisites: Point Blank Shot, Precise Shot, base attack bonus +3
Sharp-Shooting.description: Your targets only receive a +2 bonus to Armor Class due to cover, This feat has no effect against foes with no cover or total cover. (Complete Warrior)

Shield Charge.prerequisites: Improved Shield Bash, base attack bonus +3
Shield Charge.description: If you hit an opponent with your shield as part of a charge action, in addition to dealing damage normally, you may make a trip attack without provoking an attack of opportunity, if you lose, the defender does not get to try to trip you in return. (Complete Warrior)

Weakening Touch.prerequisites: Wis 17, Improved Unarmed Strike, Stunning Fist, base attack bonus +2
Weakening Touch.description: Declare that you are using this feat before you make your attack roll (thus, a missed attack roll ruins the attempt). You can make an unarmed attack that deals no damage, but instead applies a –6 penalty to the target’s Strength score for 1 minute. Multiple weakening touches delivered on the same target are not cumulative. Each attempt to deliver a weakening touch counts as one of your Stunning Fist attacks for the day. Creatures with immunity to stun effects cannot be affected by this feat. (Complete Warrior)

Anvil Of Thunder.prerequisites: Str 13, Improved Sunder, Power Attack, Two-Weapon Fighting, Weapon Focus (warhammer or light hammer), Weapon Focus (battleaxe, handaxe, or dwarven waraxe)
Anvil Of Thunder.description: You have mastered the style of fighting with hammer and axe at the same time, arid have learned to deal thunderous blows with this unique pairing of weapons. Prerequisites: Str 13, Improved Sunder, Power Attack, Two-Weapon Fighting, Weapon Focus (warhammer or light hammer), Weapon Focus (battleaxe, handaxe, or dwarven waraxe). Benefit: If you hit the same creature with both your axe and your hammer in the same round, it must make a Fortitude saving throw (DC 10 +1/2 your character level + your Strmodifier) or be dazed for 1 round. (Complete Warrior)

Bear Fang.prerequisites: Str 15, Power Attack, Two-Weapon Fighting, Weapon Focus (dagger), Weapon Focus (battleaxe, handaxe, or dwarven waraxe)
Bear Fang.description: You have mastered the fierce style of fighting with axe and dagger at the same time. You can bring the fight to close quarters in the blink of an eye. Prerequisites: Str 15, Power Attack, Two-Weapon Fighting, Weapon Focus (dagger), Weapon Focus (battleaxe, handaxe, or dwarven waraxe). Benefit: If you hit a creature with both your axe and your dagger in the same round, you deal normal damage with both weapons, and you can choose to immediately attempt to start a grapple as a free action without provoking an attack of opportunity, as if you had the improved grab ability. No initial touch attack is required. It you succeed on your grapple attempt, you drop your axe, but you immediately gain an additional attack against your grappled foe with your dagger at your highest base attack bonus (with the normal –4 penalty for attacking in a grapple). In subsequent rounds, you can use the dagger to attack while grappling at the normal penalty. (Complete Warrior)

Cavalry Charger.prerequisites: Mounted Combat, Spirited Charge, Trample, base attack bonus +6
Cavalry Charger.description: The Cavalry Charger feat enables the use of three tactical maneuvers. Unhorse: To use this maneuver, you must be mounted and charge a mounted foe. If your charge attack hits, you may make a free bull rush attempt. If the bull rush attempt succeeds, you move your foe normally, but his mount remains where it was. Leaping Charge: To use this maneuver, you must be mounted and charge a foe at least one size category smaller than your mount. Make a Ride check at the conclusion of the move portion of the charge action. Prior to making the roll, determine the DC of the check: either DC 10 for a chance to deal 2 extra points of damage or DC 20 for a chance to deal 4 extra points of damage. If you fail this Ride check, you miss your target (no attack roll) and if you fail this Ride check by 5 or more, you miss your target and fall off your mount, landing in a square adjacent to the mount’s space. Fell Trample:You can make mounted overrun attempts against more than one foe, resolving each attempt according to the rules on page 157 and 158 of the Player’s Handbook. Your mount gets a hoof attack against each foe you successfully overrun. (Complete Warrior)

Combat Brute.prerequisites: Improved Sunder, Power Attack, base attack bonus +6
Combat Brute.description: The Combat Brute feat enables the use of three tactical maneuvers. Advancing Blows: To use this maneuver, you must make a successful bull rush attempt against a foe. During the next round, all your attacks against that foe gain a +1 bonus on attack and damage rolls for each square your bull rush moved that foe. For example, if you pushed an ore back 10 (2 squares) feet with a bull rush, you would gain a +2 bonus on attack and damage rolls against that ore on the following round. Sundering Cleave: To use this maneuver, you must destroy a foe’s weapon or shield with a successful sunder attempt (see page 158 of the Player’s Handbook). If you do so, you gain an immediate additional melee attack against the foe. The additional attack is with the same weapon and at the same attack bonus as the attack that destroyed the weapon or shield. Momentum Swing: To use this maneuver, you must charge a toe in the first round, and you must make an attack using your Power Attack feat in the second round. The penalty you take on your attack roll must be –5 or worse. Your attacks during the second round gain a bonus equal to your attack roll penalty ×1-1/2, or × 3 if you’re using a two- handed weapon or a one-handed weapon wielded in two hands. For instance, if you choose to take a –6 penalty on your attack roll, you can deal an extra 9 points of damage, or an extra 18 points if you’re using a two-handed weapon or a one-handed weapon wielded in two hands. (Complete Warrior)

Crescent Moon.prerequisites: Improved Disarm, Improved Two- Weapon Fighting, Two-Weapon Fighting, Weapon Focus (dagger), Weapon Focus (bastard sword, longsword, scimitar, or short sword)
Crescent Moon.description: You have mastered the style of fighting with sword and dagger. You know how to twist an opponent’s weapons from its grasp with a single graceful motion while using your two weapons together. Prerequisites: Improved Disarm, Improved Two- Weapon Fighting, Two-Weapon Fighting, Weapon Focus (dagger), Weapon Focus (bastard sword, longsword, scimitar, or short sword). Benefit: If you hit the same creature with both your sword and your dagger in the same round, you may make an immediate disarm attempt as a free action. (Complete Warrior)

Divine Cleansing.description: You can channel energy to improve your allies’ ability to resist attacks against their vitality and health. Prerequisites: Turn or rebuke undead ability. Benefit: As a standard action, you can spend one of your turn or rebuke undead attempts to grant all allies (including yourself; within a 60-foot burst a +2 sacred bonus on fortitude saving throws for a number of rounds equal to your Charisma modifier. (Complete Warrior)

Divine Might.prerequisites: Str 13, turn or rebuke undead ability, Power Attack
Divine Might.description: You can channel energy to increase the damage you deal in combat. Prerequisites: Str 13, turn or rebuke undead ability, Power Attack. Benefit: As a free action, spend one of your turn or rebuke undead attempts to add your Charisma bonus to your weapon damage for 1 full round. (Complete Warrior)

Divine Resistance.prerequisites: Turn or rebuke undead ability, Divine Cleansing
Divine Resistance.description: You can channel energy to temporarily reduce damage you and your allies take from some sources. Prerequisites: Turn or rebuke undead ability, Divine Cleansing. Benefit: As a standard action, spend one of your turn or rebuke undead attempts to imbue all allies within a 60-foot burst (including yourself) with resistance to cold 5, electricity S, and fire 5. This resistance does not stack with similar resistances, such as those granted by spells or special abilities. The protection lasts for a number of rounds equal to your Charisma modifier. (Complete Warrior)

Divine Shield.prerequisites: Turn or rebuke undead ability, proficiency with a shield
Divine Shield.description: You can channel energy to make your shield more effective for either offense or defense. Prerequisites: Turn or rebuke undead ability, proficiency with a shield. Benefit: As a standard action, spend one of your turn/rebuke undead attempts to channel energy into your shield, granting it a bonus equal to your Charisma modifier. This bonus applies to the shield’s bonus to Armor Class and lasts for a number of rounds equal to half your character level. (Complete Warrior)

Divine Vigor.description: You can channel energy to increase your speed and durability. Prerequisites: Turn or rebuke undead ability. Benefit: As a standard action, spend one of your turn or rebuke undead attempts to increase your base speed by 10 feet and gain +2 temporary hit points per character level. These effects last a number of minutes equal to your Charisma modifier. (Complete Warrior)

Earth’s Embrace.prerequisites: Str 15, Improved Grapple or improved grab, Improved Unarmed Strike
Earth’s Embrace.description: While grappling, if you pin your opponent, you deal an extra 1d12 points of damage in each round that you maintain the pin. You must hold your opponent immobile as normal (with an opposed grapple check), but you must also remain immobile, giving opponents (other than the one you’re pinning) a +4 bonus on attack rolls against you (but you are not helpless).
